description: UMG widgets, Slate, HUD, menus, widget rendering, and UI layout issues in UE5

entries.title: Widget Not Showing / AddToViewport Not Working
entries.description: CreateWidget succeeds but the widget doesn't appear on screen after calling AddToViewport().
entries.rootCause: Common causes: widget has no visible content (empty canvas), widget visibility is set to Collapsed/Hidden in the designer, the owning Player Controller is not the local one, or the widget is added but has zero size due to missing layout (no anchors/alignment set).
solution: Verify the widget Blueprint has visible content (not just an empty Canvas Panel)
Check that Visibility is set to 'Visible' or 'HitTestInvisible' (not Collapsed/Hidden)
Ensure CreateWidget uses the correct Player Controller (GetOwningPlayer or first local player)
Check anchors and alignment in the UMG designer - widgets with no anchors may have zero size
Use 'WidgetReflector' (Window > Developer Tools > Widget Reflector) to debug widget hierarchy
Verify ZOrder - another widget might be drawn on top. Use AddToViewport(ZOrder) to control layer

entries.title: Widget Not Scaling / Looking Wrong at Different Resolutions
entries.description: UI widgets appear at the wrong size on different screen resolutions. Text or buttons are too small on 4K, or overflow the screen on lower resolutions.
entries.rootCause: The DPI Scaling Rule in Project Settings determines how widgets scale. If set incorrectly, widgets designed at one resolution won't adapt. Anchors and size boxes also affect how widgets respond to different screen sizes.
solution: Set DPI Scaling in Project Settings > User Interface > DPI Scaling Rule to 'Shortest Side'
Design your UI at a reference resolution (e.g., 1920x1080) with DPI Scale curve configured
Use Anchors to pin widgets relative to screen edges - don't use absolute positions
Use Size Box widgets to constrain min/max sizes
Use Scale Box widget to auto-scale content within a container
Test with multiple resolutions using PIE resolution settings

entries.title: Widget Binding Not Updating / Text Not Refreshing
entries.description: Widget property bindings (text, progress bar value, visibility) don't update when the underlying data changes.
entries.rootCause: Property bindings in UMG poll every frame which is expensive and may appear not to work if the bound function returns stale data. Alternatively, the binding function may not be implemented correctly or the widget's tick is disabled.
solution: Prefer event-driven updates over bindings: call SetText/SetPercent explicitly when data changes
If using bindings, ensure the bound function returns the current value (not a cached one)
Use delegates/events to push updates to the widget instead of polling
Check that the widget's Tick is enabled if you're relying on NativeTick for updates
For C++ widgets, override NativeTick or bind to model change delegates

entries.title: Widget Interaction / Click Events Not Firing
entries.description: Buttons or interactive widgets don't respond to clicks. OnClicked event never fires.
entries.rootCause: Another widget (even invisible ones) is on top blocking hit tests. The input mode is set to Game Only. The button's IsFocusable is false, or the widget's visibility is set to a non-interactive mode.
solution: Set input mode to FInputModeGameAndUI or FInputModeUIOnly when showing interactive UI
Ensure SetShowMouseCursor(true) is called on the PlayerController
Check widget hierarchy in Widget Reflector - an invisible overlay might block clicks
Set non-interactive parent widgets to 'SelfHitTestInvisible' visibility so they don't block children
Ensure the Button's 'Is Focusable' is true for keyboard/gamepad navigation
Check ZOrder - the widget might be behind another full-screen widget

entries.title: Widget Performance / Too Many Widgets Causing Lag
entries.description: UI causes frame rate drops, especially with complex widget hierarchies, many list items, or frequently updating widgets.
entries.rootCause: Slate (the underlying UI framework) recalculates layout and repaints every frame for visible widgets. Complex hierarchies, frequent text changes, and large numbers of visible widgets all contribute to CPU overhead.
solution: Use Invalidation Box widget to cache widget rendering - only repaints when explicitly invalidated
Use Retainer Box for widgets that don't change every frame (renders to texture)
For long lists, use ListView widget instead of manually creating entries in a ScrollBox
Collapse/hide widgets instead of keeping them visible but transparent
Profile with 'stat slate' to see widget count and paint time
Reduce widget tree depth - flatten hierarchies where possible

entries.title: Widget Not Receiving Gamepad / Keyboard Navigation
entries.description: UI menus can't be navigated with keyboard or gamepad. Tab/arrow keys and D-pad don't move between buttons.
entries.rootCause: UMG widgets need explicit navigation rules for non-mouse input. Buttons must be focusable, and navigation between widgets must be configured either explicitly or via automatic navigation.
solution: Set IsFocusable = true on all interactive widgets (buttons, checkboxes, etc.)
In the widget designer, set Navigation rules: Automatic, Explicit, or Custom for each widget
Call SetFocus() on the first button when the menu opens
Set the FInputModeUIOnly with the target widget to focus
For custom navigation, implement GetDesiredFocusWidget() in your widget class
Test with keyboard first (Tab + Enter), then gamepad

entries.title: 3D Widget / World Space UI Not Rendering
entries.description: A Widget Component (3D world-space UI like health bars or nameplates) doesn't render in the game world.
entries.rootCause: Widget Component requires a valid widget class set, correct draw size, and proper rendering settings. Common issues: Widget Class not assigned, draw size is zero, widget is facing away from camera, or the render target resolution is too low.
solution: Ensure Widget Class property is set on the Widget Component
Set Draw Size to appropriate values (e.g., 200x100)
For billboarding (always face camera), set Space to 'Screen' or implement custom rotation
Check that the widget component is not obscured by geometry (set render order if needed)
Increase the Widget Component's render target resolution if text appears blurry
For visibility at distance, set 'Max Interaction Distance' and check LOD settings

entries.title: CommonUI Plugin Setup Issues
entries.description: The CommonUI plugin (used by Lyra and recommended for complex UI) is not routing input correctly or activatable widgets don't activate/deactivate as expected.
entries.rootCause: CommonUI requires specific setup: a CommonGameViewportClient subclass, proper widget activation/deactivation patterns, and its own input routing that overrides the standard UMG input flow.
solution: Set GameViewportClientClass in DefaultEngine.ini to your CommonGameViewportClient subclass
Use UCommonActivatableWidget as base class for all menu panels
Push/pop widgets using ActivateWidget/DeactivateWidget instead of AddToViewport/RemoveFromViewport
Configure CommonUI Input Action Data Tables for gamepad back/confirm actions
Check that your widget implements GetDesiredInputConfig() correctly
Review Lyra's UI implementation as reference for CommonUI patterns
